compiled_state.title: capture-review-diff.sh produces empty hash for untracked files, causing review gate mismatch
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
When capture-review-diff.sh runs on a diff that includes untracked (not yet staged) files, it produces an empty diff hash (e3b0c44298fc — SHA-256 of empty string). The review gate then records this empty hash in review-status. When the commit runs, the pre-commit hook computes the actual diff hash (which includes the new file content) and finds a mismatch, blocking the commit.

Workaround: run git add before capture-review-diff.sh so the new file is staged and included in the diff.

Fix: capture-review-diff.sh should detect untracked files in the working tree and either (a) include them in the diff computation, or (b) error with a clear message instructing the user to stage first.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). capture-review-diff.sh produces empty hash for untracked files.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fix: added untracked file detection to capture-review-diff.sh. When only untracked files exist (no staged/unstaged changes), warns user to git add before review instead of silently falling back to HEAD~1 diff.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
